_meta: WO-FS shared config — the single home for both feature-selection loops (docs/XGB-ZLECENIE-LOOP.md + docs/LSTM-ZLECENIE-LOOP.md). Keys starting with _ are documentation and are stripped by the loader.

PARALLEL._note: Full-resources use: each XGB/LSTM training saturates its thread count; cache-warm fans out over `warm_workers` processes. Counts are CAPPED at os.cpu_count()-1 at runtime and overridable via FS_NTHREAD/FS_TORCH_THREADS, so moving to a smaller server auto-adapts. Fixed thread count -> reproducible at that count. demo stays light so gates are fast. `torch_threads` = threads PER WORKER in fan-out LSTM stages (cpcv/loop/study2: many workers, few threads each); `torch_threads_single` = threads for a SINGLE-training LSTM stage (strategy/holdout: one heavy fit, take the whole box) -- see BEST_PRACTICE_FOR_ML_LOOPS_ON_THIS_SERVER.md sec.4/8.

SUPERVISOR._note: Detached CONVERGENCE optimizer (make fs-search-on UNIVERSE=full) — NO wall-clock budget. Freezes labels ONCE, then optimizes XGB to convergence, then LSTM to convergence, then writes all_converged.flag and EXITS. A model has converged when the best CV macro-F1 stops improving by > min_gain for `patience` rounds AND the selected feature set is unchanged AND no new Sonnet feature was accepted. Resumable across a server change (skips converged models). NEVER reads the holdout.

STORES._note: 1h from the xgb store (FS_BARS_1H env overrides; demo fallback), 1d NATIVE from the lstm store (WO-XGB §1 forbids 1h->1d resampling), 1w aggregated from native 1d.

SPLITS._note: warmup = indicator lookbacks only; CV = the only window any decision may use; holdout read exactly once (guarded by artifacts/<universe>/holdout_used.flag). Same holdout window as the sealed v2 pipelines.

STUDY1._note: Label calibration (shared truth for BOTH models): CUSUM threshold, EWMA vol span, barrier multiples, horizon. Objective = mean macro-F1 of the fixed reference XGB on Purged K-Fold. min_class_frac prunes degenerate label geometries.

XGB_LOOP._note: Backward elimination with correlation clustering + clustered MDA (decision) and treeshap (agreement control). Stop rules per WO §4.6; selected set = smallest within 1 SE, then stability pi >= 0.7 over bootstraps. ECONOMY: feature SELECTION (loop + stability) runs on a representative ticker subsample (select_ticker_subsample) — the clustered-MDA permutations over the full 503-asset panel are the cost, and a subsample gives the same feature ranking; Study 2 / CPCV / SHAP / holdout then use ALL tickers.

XGB_STUDY2._note: Ranges copied from xgb/src/config/xgboost_optuna_search_space.json (the audited v2 space); objective becomes multi:softprob num_class=3, trial metric = mean fold macro-F1, pruning on fold mlogloss.

LSTM._note: Fixed base architecture for the channel-elimination loop (WO-LSTM §3); Study 2 tunes it afterwards. ECONOMY mode (WO §4.2): channel selection runs on a representative ticker subsample with few epochs; the FINALIST (Study-2 refit / CPCV / holdout) trains on all tickers, full epochs, >=3 seeds (WO §6). seq_len_max fixes event eligibility so every Study-2 L stays on the same event set.

TRADING._note: event returns bucketed by exit day for the Sharpe/DSR final evaluation
